title: alnus borealis
intro: Alnus borealis, also known as the green alder, is a deciduous shrub or small tree native to the boreal forests of North America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can be attributed to the presence of compounds such as flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Alnus borealis include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve respiratory health, and protect against oxidative stress, making it a popular ingredient in herbal remedies for colds and coughs. Alnus borealis is often prepared as a tea or infusion, and its leaves and bark can also be used to make tinctures and salves for topical application.
